ip_outcome_note: No suspected_ip flag from vision pass; the depicted horror is a generic Duskmourn nightmare creature, not a named lore character.

trivia_bullets.text: Duskmourn: House of Horror (no. DSK) is the 102nd Magic expansion, released 2024-09-27. The set is top-down designed around modern horror, drawing on 70s/80s/90s slasher cinema and haunted-house tropes — Wizards' Vision Design Handoff explicitly cites suspense-over-shock, the Amityville Horror, and Freddy/Jason-type unkillable antagonists as influences [Scryfall set page; Wizards Vision Design Handoff, via WebSearch snippet; MTG Wiki: Duskmourn].
sources: Wizards: Duskmourn Vision Design Handoff (via WebSearch snippet)

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "We set it on fire. It lived. We cut off its head. It lived. We fell to it, one by one. It lives." —Sunrise, survivor sentry [Scryfall flavor_text]. The escalation-by-failed-kill cadence is a direct slasher-villain trope ("the killer always comes back"), and the speaker is a flavor-text-only Survivor — the set's Survivor creature type is tied to the Survival mechanic, primarily in green/white, where tapped creatures get rewarded for outliving the turn [MTG Wiki: Survivor, via WebSearch snippet].

Horrid Vigor is a colorshift of Offer Immortality (Phyrexia: All Will Be One no. 102 / Foundations no. 525), which is the black {1}{B} instant with identical text. Horrid Vigor moves the deathtouch+indestructible package into green, a color that historically pays for deathtouch but rarely gets it stapled to indestructible — and is also strictly better than Serpent's Gift (Magic 2013 no. 190, {2}{G}, deathtouch-only) [Scryfall; WebSearch snippet citing card comparisons].

Art by Chris Cold, who illustrated several major Duskmourn pieces including Valgavoth, Harrower of Souls (Duskmourn Commander no. 6) and Dollmaker's Shop // Porcelain Gallery (DSK no. 4) — placing this common in the same painterly horror visual language as the set's marquee demon-lord [Scryfall artist credit; mtg.wtf artist page, via WebSearch snippet].

EDHREC rank 5672; appears in 24,174 Commander decks per EDHREC's index — meaningful for a common combat trick, suggesting it's earned green-deathtouch-package inclusion in casual brews despite no keyword on the card itself [Scryfall edhrec_rank; EDHREC card page meta description].
